homepage.title: Matthew Pennycook - Serving Greenwich and Woolwich
homepage.meta_description: As MP for Greenwich and Woolwich, I champion the interests of the communities that I serve in Westminster as well as in my position as Shadow Housing Minister.
headings: h3: My constituency: Greenwich and Woolwich
homepage.text_excerpt: Matthew Pennycook - Serving Greenwich and Woolwich Skip to content Matthew Pennycook Labour MP for Greenwich and Woolwich Menu Close About me Help for constituents General enquiries Contact me Dear constituents, Being the MP for Greenwich and Woolwich is the biggest responsibility I will ever have. As your MP, my focus is on championing the interests of all the communities that I serve in Westminster as well as in my position as Minister of State for Housing and Planning. I look forward to continuing to work with you to build a better future – in Greenwich and Woolwich and beyond. Matthew Pennycook MP The latest from Matthew Follow me on X Follow me on BlueSky Like my Facebook page Subscribe to my YouTube channel My constituency: Greenwich and Woolwich I represent the parliamentary constituency of Greenwich and Woolwich (which covers parts but not all of the Royal Borough of Greenwich). The constituency is made up of the communities of Woolwich, Charlton, Greenwich and parts of Blackheath and Plumstead, each of which retain their own distinct character and community spirit. © Matthew Pennycook, 2025. | Office Standards Policy | Privacy policy | X | BlueSky | Facebook | YouTube Proudly powered by WordPress .

pages.title: About me - Matthew Pennycook
pages.meta_description: I was born in October 1982 and raised, along with my younger brother, in South London by my mum. I went to the local primary, then the local state secondary, before becoming the first person in my family to go to university. My secondary school was not one that typically sent many of its pupils,… Continue reading About me
pages.text_excerpt: About me - Matthew Pennycook Skip to content Matthew Pennycook Labour MP for Greenwich and Woolwich Menu Close About me Help for constituents General enquiries Contact me About me I was born in October 1982 and raised, along with my younger brother, in South London by my mum. I went to the local primary, then the local state secondary, before becoming the first person in my family to go to university. My secondary school was not one that typically sent many of its pupils, if any, to university each year, let alone one that pushed them to consider applying to any of our country’s elite universities. I will never forget one senior teacher’s advice to me upon expressing an interest in applying: “go for it by all means, but don’t put down anywhere stupid like the London School of Economics as one of your preferences.” So that’s exactly what I did. With the support of several outstanding teachers, I secured my place at the LSE to study History and International Relations. Like many people who head to university from an ordinary background, I always had a feeling that I didn’t really belong. But I worked hard, did well and was lucky enough to secure funding for an MPhil at Balliol College, Oxford, in the same subject. When I left university, I went to work for a number of charities and voluntary sector organisations. I got involved with the Child Poverty Action Group to ensure low-income families and children get a better deal. I worked for the Fair Pay Network to raise awareness about in-work poverty and push for action to address Britain’s endemic levels of low pay. And I became a researcher at the Resolution Foundation to develop new ideas about how the enormous wealth our country generates can be more shared more fairly. I have been lucky. But I know that any success I’ve had has been achieved despite our unequal society not because of it. That’s why I decided to join the Labour Party. I was determined to be a voice for those who do not have privileged access to power and to be part of a movement dedicated to bringing about a fairer and more equal society than the one I grew up in. In Greenwich, I became a local councillor, serving the area that has been home to Joanna and me for many years and is now home to our two children, Arthur and Edie. I was also a Governor at James Wolfe Primary School in West Greenwich and served on the Greenwich Housing Rights’ Board of Trustees. When our constituency’s long-serving MP Nick Raynsford decided to stand down at the 2015 election, I put myself forward to succeed him. It was a huge honour to be elected in May 2015 to represent our constituency in Parliament – one I would never take for granted. I’m extremely grateful to all those who have put their trust in me over the years and I am committed to continuing to work tirelessly on behalf of the people of Greenwich and Woolwich. © Matthew Pennycook, 2025. | Office Standards Policy | Privacy policy | X | BlueSky | Facebook | YouTube Proudly powered by WordPress .

pages.title: Get help with an issue - Matthew Pennycook
pages.meta_description: I can help constituents with a wide range of issues but, in many cases, others are best placed to assist. Read 'How I can help' to ensure I am the right person to contact. MPs only deal with enquiries and cases on behalf of their own constituents. Make sure I am your MP before sending… Continue reading Get help with an issue
pages.text_excerpt: Get help with an issue - Matthew Pennycook Skip to content Matthew Pennycook Labour MP for Greenwich and Woolwich Menu Close About me Help for constituents General enquiries Contact me Get help with an issue I can help constituents with a wide range of issues but, in many cases, others are best placed to assist. Read ‘ How I can help ‘ to ensure I am the right person to contact. MPs only deal with enquiries and cases on behalf of their own constituents. Make sure I am your MP before sending your enquiry . Please choose the issue you need help with: Select an issue Immigration (status, applications, ability to enter the UK) Passports (delays) Housing (eviction, quality, suitability, access to) Health (including mental health) Welfare (changes in benefits payments and entitlements, disability allowances) Employment (issues with employer, dismissal) Community issues (crime and anti-social behaviour, safety, neighbours, noise) Our streets (dirt, noise, traffic, planning) Schools (access to, issues with) Transport (quality of public, congestion, policy) Public services (initiative under threat) Bills (unfair utility bills, council tax) Request an appointment with Matthew Something else (my issue doesn’t fit into one of these categories) Immigration E.g. immigration status, applications, ability to enter the UK Passport applications E.g. a delay Housing E.g. eviction, quality, suitability or access to housing Health (including mental health Welfare E.g. changes in benefit payments and entitlements or disability allowances Employment E.g. issues with an employer, such as discrimination or dismissal Community issues E.g. crime and anti-social behaviour, safety, neighbours or noise Our streets E.g. dirt, noise, traffic, planning Schools and education E.g. access to or issues with a school Transport E.g. quality of public transport, traffic and congestion, transport policy Public services E.g. a local service or initiative is under threat Bills E.g. unfair utility or council tax bills Something else --> If you are a constituent and you feel your issue would be better handled in person, get in touch to request an appointment . © Matthew Pennycook, 2025. | Office Standards Policy | Privacy policy | X | BlueSky | Facebook | YouTube Proudly powered by WordPress .

pages.title: General enquiries - Matthew Pennycook
pages.meta_description: If you need to get in touch with Matthew Pennycook, please use the form below.
pages.text_excerpt: General enquiries - Matthew Pennycook Skip to content Matthew Pennycook Labour MP for Greenwich and Woolwich Menu Close About me Help for constituents General enquiries Contact me General enquiries If you have an enquiry about anything else, please get in touch using the form below. If your enquiry is about a constituency matter, please make sure I am your MP . Parliamentary protocol dictates that MPs may only deal with constituency enquiries and cases on behalf of their own constituents.
